user_query:
Hi team—As the product manager for our onboarding flow, I want to finalize user‑facing validation and error messages before the next frontend release.

Please use the provided inputs to produce three deliverables under the workspace:
1) out/message_rewrites.json
2) out/frequency_summary.csv
3) out/rewrite_notes.md

Inputs:
- input/raw_messages.json (source of current message codes, severities, and text)
- input/tone_guide.md (tone and style guidance you must follow)
- tools/simulate_validation_run.py (a deterministic script that prints sample validation output to stdout)

Requirements:
- Run: python tools/simulate_validation_run.py
  • Parse only lines in the format "<SEVERITY> <CODE>: <message>" from stdout.
  • Compute hit_count per CODE from this command output. Use this strictly for counts/prioritization; do not copy message text from the simulation.
- Rewrite every message in input/raw_messages.json using the tone and style rules in input/tone_guide.md.
  • Keep each rewritten_text concise (<= 120 characters), actionable, and non‑blaming.
  • Preserve meaning and the user’s next step. Do not introduce new codes.
- Create out/message_rewrites.json as an array of objects with these fields for every code in input/raw_messages.json:
  • code (string)
  • severity (string; take from input/raw_messages.json)
  • original_text (string; from input/raw_messages.json)
  • rewritten_text (string; your rewrite <= 120 chars)
  • rationale (string; 1–2 sentences on your changes for tone/clarity)
  • hit_count (integer; from the parsed command output; 0 if not observed)
  • appears_in_simulation (boolean; true if code appeared in the command output at least once)
- Create out/frequency_summary.csv with columns: code,severity,hit_count, covering all codes in input/raw_messages.json (hit_count = 0 if absent in the simulation).
- Create out/rewrite_notes.md with:
  • A short summary of the tone rules you applied (based on input/tone_guide.md).
  • The three most frequent codes from the simulation (by hit_count) and a one‑line note on how your rewrites improve them.
  • Any discrepancies you noticed (e.g., if a code’s severity in the simulation log differs from input/raw_messages.json), if any.
  • A final line stating how many rewritten_text values exceed 120 characters (should be 0).

Acceptance:
- All codes in input/raw_messages.json appear exactly once in out/message_rewrites.json and out/frequency_summary.csv.
- hit_count values in both outputs match the parsed command output from tools/simulate_validation_run.py.
- Every rewritten_text follows input/tone_guide.md and is <= 120 characters. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
